canonical_definition: Across the surveyed literature, Optical Character Recognition (OCR) is most commonly defined as the observable behavior of identifying, extracting, and transcribing text from visual media such as images, documents, and video frames. The application of this task is shown to be diverse, encompassing general 'scene text recognition', text within user interfaces ('Element OCR', 'Heading OCR'), and specialized domains like historical documents, which are further divided into 'Handwritten text recognition' and 'Printed text recognition'. This behavior is predominantly operationalized and measured using a range of visual question answering benchmarks. The most frequently cited instruments for evaluating OCR are TextVQA and DocVQA, while other papers also employ OCRBench, ChartQA, ST-VQA, and OCRVQA to assess this capability. While the prevailing usage treats OCR as a task, a smaller stream of research frames it as a latent 'OCR ability,' 'capability,' or 'perception,' sometimes specifying 'fine-grained text recognition' for small or blurry text. Studies highlight the difficulty of this task, noting challenges in "accurately distinguishing bewildering OCR cases" such as similar handwritten words ("Seeing the Image: Prioritizing Visual Correlation by Contrastive Alignment"). Some work posits a directional relationship, suggesting that a model's underlying visual perception influences its performance on OCR tasks. To perform this behavior, some studies report using specific tools like the Pytesseract or EasyOCR libraries.

definitions.text: The task of identifying and extracting text from images or documents.
definitions.paper: Visual CoT: Advancing Multi-Modal Language Models with a Comprehensive Dataset and Benchmark for Chain-of-Thought Reasoning

definitions.text: Reading and transcribing text that appears in images or screenshots.
definitions.paper: WildVision: Evaluating Vision-Language Models in the Wild with Human Preferences

definitions.text: Recognizing and reading text within an image region, often treated as an out-of-domain task for referring models.
definitions.paper: ControlMLLM: Training-Free Visual Prompt Learning for Multimodal Large Language Models

definitions.text: The task of identifying and extracting specific, predefined types of information from unstructured text.
definitions.paper: Smoothie: Label Free Language Model Routing

definitions.text: Extracting the text content from a specific UI element highlighted by a bounding box on a webpage screenshot.
definitions.paper: Harnessing Webpage UIs for Text-Rich Visual Understanding

definitions.text: Identifying and extracting the textual content of headlines or titles from a webpage.
definitions.paper: Harnessing Webpage UIs for Text-Rich Visual Understanding

definitions.text: The task of performing Optical Character Recognition to read and interpret text that appears within video frames.
definitions.paper: Frame-Voyager: Learning to Query Frames for Video Large Language Models

definitions.text: The observable task of identifying and classifying characters from images of handwritten letters.
definitions.paper: SCISSOR: Mitigating Semantic Bias through Cluster-Aware Siamese Networks for Robust Classification

definitions.text: The observable behavior of extracting and understanding textual content embedded within an image to perform a task.
definitions.paper: Personality Matters: User Traits PredictLLMPreferences in Multi-Turn Collaborative Tasks

definitions.text: Extracting visible text elements from a chart without semantic structuring.
definitions.paper: LegalSearchLM: Rethinking Legal Case Retrieval as Legal Elements Generation

definitions.text: Transcribing text from images of handwritten historical documents, accounting for diverse handwriting styles, abbreviations, and degradation.
definitions.paper: Turning Logic Against Itself: Probing Model Defenses Through Contrastive Questions

definitions.text: Transcribing text from images of printed historical documents, handling archaic fonts, layout variations, and orthographic inconsistencies.
definitions.paper: Turning Logic Against Itself: Probing Model Defenses Through Contrastive Questions

definitions.text: The process of identifying and converting textual content from image frames of presentation slides into machine-readable text, using OCR or vision-language models.
definitions.paper: Sparse Neurons Carry Strong Signals of Question Ambiguity inLLMs

definitions.text: The latent capacity to recognize and extract text from images or documents.
definitions.paper: What matters when building vision-language models?

definitions.text: The ability to accurately identify, read, and transcribe text present within an image.
definitions.paper: Seeing the Image: Prioritizing Visual Correlation by Contrastive Alignment

definitions.text: The specific facet of visual perception involving the detection and recognition of text characters within an image.
definitions.paper: ConvBench: A Multi-Turn Conversation Evaluation Benchmark with Hierarchical Ablation Capability for Large Vision-Language Models

definitions.text: The ability to identify digits or characters from visual input, including handwritten or natural-image digit recognition.
definitions.paper: UniBench: Visual Reasoning Requires Rethinking Vision-Language Beyond Scaling

definitions.text: The latent ability to read and interpret small, blurred, or text-dense visual content in images.
definitions.paper: Feast Your Eyes:  Mixture-of-Resolution Adaptation for Multimodal Large Language Models

definitions.text: The latent ability to recognize and align text present in document images with query terms or document content.
definitions.paper: ColPali: Efficient Document Retrieval with Vision Language Models
